title: quercus lanuginosa var. oxycarpa
intro: Quercus lanuginosa var. oxycarpa, also known as the Chinese white oak, is a deciduous tree species native to China and parts of Southeast Asia, characterized by its distinctive white hairs on the underside of its leaves. The plant has been used in traditional Chinese medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects. The health benefits of Quercus lanuginosa var. oxycarpa include its ability to reduce fever, relieve cough and sore throat, and treat respiratory issues such as bronchitis and pneumonia. In herbal preparations, the tree's bark and leaves are often used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures that can be used to support respiratory health and alleviate symptoms of colds and flu.
